title: HtmlQualityReview
description: Output of the post-render html_quality stage. Codex (gpt-5.5) reads the rendered review.html, emits a fixed copy and a list of every change it made. Strict JSON, OpenAI-strict-compatible.

fixed_html.description: The corrected HTML document. If no changes were necessary this is the original HTML byte-for-byte.

location.description: Where in the document the change applies (e.g., 'h1 title', 'section #methods anchor', 'figure caption near eq:3'). Free text.

issue.description: What was wrong. e.g., 'literal LaTeX layout commands surfaced as text', 'broken table cell alignment', 'orphaned section anchor'.

before.description: The original HTML fragment, truncated to a readable snippet. Null when the fix is structural (e.g., adding a missing element).

after.description: The rewritten HTML fragment. Null when the fix is a deletion.

severity.description: Reader-impact severity. 'critical' = unreadable / wrong content; 'major' = renders but obviously broken; 'minor' = cosmetic; 'info' = a note about something acceptable.

rationale.description: One sentence justifying the change so a moderator can audit.

summary.description: One-paragraph summary of what was fixed and what (if anything) the moderator should still double-check by eye.

confidence.description: Codex's confidence that the rewrite preserved meaning. Below 0.7 the moderator should diff before publishing.
